diagnostic_only_disclaimer: Portfolio X-Ray v2 is diagnostic-only. It summarizes existing report pipeline outputs and in-memory diagnostics; it does not optimize, change weights, change mandate gates, change stress pass/fail status, score portfolios, select portfolios, or provide trade instructions.

key_points: Largest holding: SPY at 45.000% of capital.
Asset class mix (top buckets): equity 45.000%, fixed_income 45.000%, commodity 10.000%.

Top three holdings sum to 90.000% (high diagnostic threshold 65.000%).

portfolio_behavior_snapshot.headline: The portfolio delivered 0.080 CAGR with a -0.220 maximum drawdown over the primary diagnostic window.
key_points: Realized CAGR 0.080 with annualized volatility 0.100 over the primary window.
Maximum drawdown -0.220; deepest episode recovered within sample.
Daily historical VaR/ES tail metrics are available for this run.
Base-benchmark beta 0.850 on monthly aligned returns.

data_quality_warnings: Correlation breakdown is limited because the primary-window correlation matrix is missing.

limitations: Taxonomy is annotation-only in V1 and does not select assets or change weights.
ETF sector and currency fields can describe broad economic exposure rather than exact look-through holdings.

items.method: monthly simple portfolio metrics per metrics_specification.md

limitations: Historical metrics describe realized behavior in the selected window, not expected future returns.
VaR and ES use historical daily simple returns (not Monte Carlo); check against stress scenarios before action.
When tail_risk.metric_available is false, flat var/es fields may be absent or stale.
risk_diagnostics.method: monthly simple portfolio metrics (metrics_spec); daily historical VaR/ES when tail_risk is present

limitations: Factor exposures are diagnostics from the existing factor pipeline and do not replace raw holdings.
Kalman betas are current-regime diagnostics and do not replace raw 5Y/10Y OLS betas.
Reported significance uses HAC/Newey-West p-values and confidence intervals per stress_testing_spec §8.4; classical OLS t/p in by_factor are diagnostic-only.
factor_exposure.method: weekly OLS portfolio factor betas (5Y/10Y); Kalman latest; variance decomposition; factor_regression inference panel (HAC, multicollinearity, residual diagnostics)

items.name: hidden_equity_beta
items.interpretation: The portfolio has meaningful equity-market sensitivity even if holdings labels look diversified.
items.next_test: Check equity_shock, recession_severe, and downside equity behavior.
items.limitation: Beta is historical and can change when correlations or holdings change.

items.name: duration_concentration
items.fact: Intermediate/long duration-labeled holdings are 45.0% of capital.
items.interpretation: The portfolio may be sensitive to rates and duration shocks beyond broad asset labels.
items.next_test: Check rates_shock, real-rate beta, and stress RC contribution from bond holdings.
items.limitation: Duration bucket is taxonomy metadata, not a live duration estimate.

items.name: credit_concentration
items.interpretation: Credit-labeled capital concentration is not elevated under X-Ray rules.
items.next_test: Review credit factor beta and credit stress scenarios if HY/IG holdings exist.
items.limitation: Low labeled credit weight does not remove spread or default risk.

items.name: liquidity_concentration
items.interpretation: Liquidity-labeled concentration is not elevated under taxonomy rules.
items.next_test: Still review liquidity_shock if holdings are illiquid in practice.
items.limitation: Absence of a liquidity label does not guarantee market liquidity.

items.name: correlation_or_common_factor_concentration
items.fact: Raw covariance PCA PC1 explains 66.0% of asset-return variance.
items.interpretation: Several holdings may be driven by the same statistical risk direction.
items.next_test: Review PCA loadings, raw correlation PCA, and residual PCA after factor removal.
items.limitation: PCA is statistical and does not identify a tradable economic factor by itself.

items.name: residual_pca_concentration
items.fact: Residual PCA PC1 explains 52.0% of factor-adjusted return variance.
items.interpretation: After named factors, holdings may still share a common residual direction.
items.next_test: Review residual PCA loadings and unexplained factor residual share.
items.limitation: Residual PCA is model-dependent on the factor set used.

items.name: weak_hedge_behavior
items.fact: hedge-labeled assets (GLD) did not offset portfolio loss in worst scenario recession_severe; factor OOS mean absolute error (5Y beta) is 3.0%.
items.interpretation: Defensive or hedge-labeled sleeves may not provide expected crisis offset.
items.next_test: Review historical episodes, hedge asset stress PnL, and crisis_hedge role coverage.
items.limitation: Hedge labels and factor OOS tests are approximations, not live hedge effectiveness.

items.name: tail_risk
items.fact: Daily historical ES95 is -3.0% (historical, daily, window 10y).
items.interpretation: Left-tail loss magnitude is elevated relative to configured ES thresholds.
items.next_test: Review tail_risk block, stress worst-case loss, and drawdown metrics together.
items.limitation: Historical VaR/ES is backward-looking and not a forecast.

items.name: stress_loss_contributor_concentration
items.fact: In worst scenario recession_severe, top stress RC asset HYG is 42.0%.
items.interpretation: Stress losses may be dominated by a small number of assets.
items.next_test: Inspect pnl_by_asset_pct and Top3 RC for the worst stress scenario.
items.limitation: Synthetic stress RC uses scenario covariance diagnostics and is not a pass/fail gate.

items.name: macro_factor_dependency
items.fact: Largest |beta| is beta_eq=0.720; dominant variance share equity=40.0%; residual share 70.0%.
items.interpretation: Portfolio behavior may be driven by a small set of macro/factor exposures.
items.next_test: Review factor exposure section, factor stress history, and residual risk.
items.limitation: Factor maps are linear approximations and may miss regime shifts.

items.name: single_asset_risk_concentration
items.interpretation: Portfolio risk is more concentrated than capital weights alone imply.
items.next_test: Compare weight vs RC_vol and inspect stress Top1/Top3 RC concentration.
items.limitation: RC_vol depends on the selected covariance window and existing RC calculation.

limitations: Hidden risk flags are transparent threshold rules, not AI judgment and not portfolio recommendations.
Each category reports flagged, below-threshold, or unavailable evidence; absence of a flag is not proof of safety.

items.conflict_summary: Primary label Equity Growth Portfolio coexists with high equity crash weakness — the label describes holdings, not immunity to that regime.

limitations: Archetype is a rule-based behavior label with caveats, not a portfolio selection decision.
A portfolio can have multiple simultaneous characteristics; confidence reflects signal clarity and regime tensions.
Negative evidence and conflicting signals can apply even when a primary label is shown.
RC_vol is contribution to portfolio variance from the existing pipeline and is diagnostic-only.
Stress loss contribution is scenario-specific and should not be read as expected loss.
risk_budget_view.method: RC_vol percentage variance contribution; worst-case stress PnL by asset across scenarios

limitations: Weakness Map aggregates existing evidence only; it is not a forecasting or scoring model.
Low severity with adverse_evidence=false means thresholds were not crossed, not that the risk is absent.
crypto_shock appears only when crypto exposure is present in taxonomy or weights.
volatility_spike is factor-only (beta_vix, historical ES_95); no synthetic stress scenario is mapped by design (RM-948 Option B).
weakness_map.method: rule-based threshold aggregation over stress, factor, tail, and metrics evidence
weakness_map.window: stress scenarios (synthetic); 5Y weekly factor betas; portfolio metrics 10Y (120M)

risk_contribution_summary.method_note: RC_vol is diagnostic-only and does not act as an optimizer gate or recommendation rule.
